(born April 28, 1984) is a Canadian [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") recording artist and TV personality. She was a top six finalist on the [fifth season](/wiki/Canadian_Idol_(Season_5) "Canadian Idol (Season 5)") of *[Canadian Idol](/wiki/Canadian_Idol "Canadian Idol")* in 2007. In 2009, Oram received the "Rising Star" [Canadian Country Music Award](/wiki/Canadian_Country_Music_Award "Canadian Country Music Award") and was nominated for a [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award"), for Country Album of the Year, for her debut album *Chasing the Sun*.

Lindsey Ward (August 14, 2007). ["Oram booted from 'Canadian Idol'"](http://jam.canoe.ca/Television/TV_Shows/C/Canadian_Idol/2007/08/14/4418454-sun.html). *Canoe*. Musical artist

**Tara Oram** (born April 28, 1984) is a Canadian [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") recording artist and TV personality. She was a top six finalist on the [fifth season](/wiki/Canadian_Idol_(Season_5) "Canadian Idol (Season 5)") of *[Canadian Idol](/wiki/Canadian_Idol "Canadian Idol")* in 2007. In 2009, Oram received the "Rising Star" [Canadian Country Music Award](/wiki/Canadian_Country_Music_Award "Canadian Country Music Award") and was nominated for a [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award"), for Country Album of the Year, for her debut album *Chasing the Sun*.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tara_Oram&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Oram was born in [Hare Bay](/wiki/Hare_Bay,_Newfoundland_and_Labrador "Hare Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador"), [Newfoundland](/wiki/Newfoundland_and_Labrador "Newfoundland and Labrador") but moved frequently during her early years. Oram first moved to the [Malton](/wiki/Milton,_Ontario "Milton, Ontario") area of [Mississauga, Ontario](/wiki/Mississauga,_Ontario "Mississauga, Ontario") when she was 10 years old, and later to [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), where she attended [Bramalea Secondary School](/wiki/Bramalea_Secondary_School "Bramalea Secondary School").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tara_Oram&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

At age 16 Oram was signed to indie label Wellcraft Music Group and released her first country music single called, "More Than I Dreamed" which was her first top 50 hit. She later toured with a local agency called Performerz, which allowed her to sing in theatres, malls and festivals throughout Ontario. When 19 Oram joined her first real band, Big Catch. The band played the bar/club circuit and corporate events around Ontario for the better part of four years, performing a mix of country, top 40, rock, R'n B and retro. In 2007, Oram auditioned for the hit show [Canadian Idol](/wiki/Canadian_Idol "Canadian Idol"), earning a 6th-place finish on the hit reality show.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The next year, Oram signed her first major record deal with Open Road/Universal Music Canada and also appeared on reality TV series called, "The Tara Diaries" on [CMT](/wiki/Country_Music_Television "Country Music Television").[[2]](#cite_note-2) CMT filmed a Christmas special she hosted, "The Night Before Christmas".[[3]](#cite_note-3) In October 2008, Oram's first show as a signed artist was opening for her Idol, [Marty Stuart](/wiki/Marty_Stuart "Marty Stuart") in Halifax, Nova Scotia.

In 2009, CMT and [YTV](/wiki/YTV_(Canadian_TV_channel) "YTV (Canadian TV channel)") Oram appeared a celebrity judge on the TV series, "Karaoke Star Junior".[[4]](#cite_note-4)

On July 10, 2010, Oram was the opening act for [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift") and performed at the second annual [Cavendish Beach Music Festival](/wiki/Cavendish_Beach_Music_Festival "Cavendish Beach Music Festival") in [Cavendish](/wiki/Cavendish,_Prince_Edward_Island "Cavendish, Prince Edward Island"), Prince Edward Island, singing to a crowd of more than 35,000 people.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Oram became a judge on [YTV's](/wiki/YTV_(Canadian_TV_channel) "YTV (Canadian TV channel)") Canadian reality TV show, [The Next Star](/wiki/The_Next_Star "The Next Star") since Season 5, which premiered July 18, 2012.

### Awards and nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tara_Oram&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards and nominations")]

Oram received nominations for the 2009 East Coast Music Awards in the categories Female Solo Recording of the Year and Video of the Year, and won in the Country Recording of the Year award.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
She also received a CRMA (Canadian Radio Music Award) nomination as Best New Group/Solo Artist.
On February 2, 2009 Oram was nominated for a Juno Award in the Country Recording of the Year category.
Oram won the 2009 CCMA Rising Star award. She was also nominated for Rising Star, Female Artist of the Year and Video of the Year.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2015, Tara Oram received a Star on Brampton's Walk of Fame.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
